headline: Sellers Claim the Close as Homebuilders and Healthcare Bear the Brunt
body_html:
Friday's session ended on a distinctly defensive note, with declining issues outpacing advancers by more than two to one across the 476-name universe — 326 stocks finishing in the red against just 147 in the green. The damage was broad but not evenly distributed: Homebuilders absorbed the sharpest blow, surrendering more than three percent as a group, while Healthcare, Industrials, and Materials each shed better than one percent. Energy was the lone pocket of resilience, edging fractionally higher as geopolitical tension in the Gulf kept crude sentiment elevated.

The session's most dramatic single move belonged to Intuitive Surgical, which tumbled 14.15 percent, making it the steepest decliner in the universe by a wide margin. The medical-device giant's slide dragged Healthcare lower and reverberated into broader sentiment. Electronic-design automation names compounded the software sector's weakness, with Cadence Design falling 9.47 percent and Synopsys retreating 7.85 percent in tandem.

Against that backdrop, Sonos provided a rare bright spot, surging 5.89 percent to lead all gainers, followed closely by Seagate Technology at 5.66 percent. As traders head into the weekend, escalating activity in the Strait of Hormuz and a fluid geopolitical landscape leave energy markets and risk appetite alike in an uneasy balance.

briefs.headline: Intuitive Surgical's Steep Drop Rattles Medical-Device Names
briefs.body: Intuitive Surgical plunged 14.15 percent on Friday, the largest single-session decline in the covered universe, and pulled STAAR Surgical down 8.76 percent alongside it. The twin selloff dragged Healthcare to among the worst-performing sectors of the day, underscoring how concentrated earnings or guidance disappointments can ripple rapidly through a tight subsector.

briefs.headline: EDA Software Pair Hit Hard as Software Sector Slides
briefs.body: Cadence Design Systems and Synopsys fell 9.47 percent and 7.85 percent, respectively, on Friday, weighing heavily on the Software sector's 0.96 percent decline. The electronic-design automation names have benefited in recent quarters from surging chip-design activity, making the simultaneous retreat notable heading into a pivotal earnings stretch.

briefs.headline: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Drops as Gulf Tensions Escalate
briefs.body: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have declined measurably as hostilities between the United States and Iran intensify across the Gulf region, according to reports. The chokepoint handles a substantial share of global seaborne oil, and any sustained disruption threatens supply chains far beyond the immediate conflict zone.

briefs.headline: Boeing Regains Authority to Certify Its Own Airworthiness
briefs.body: The Federal Aviation Administration restored Boeing's ability to self-certify airworthiness on its 737 Max and 787 Dreamliner programs, a significant regulatory milestone for the planemaker. The decision marks a reversal of oversight restrictions imposed following earlier safety scrutiny and could streamline delivery timelines that have weighed on airline customers.

briefs.headline: Sonos and Seagate Lead a Thin Group of Friday Winners
briefs.body: Consumer-electronics maker Sonos jumped 5.89 percent to top the day's gainers list, while data-storage specialist Seagate Technology followed with a 5.66 percent advance. The moves stood out against a heavily negative tape and suggested stock-specific catalysts rather than any broad risk-on rotation were doing the lifting.

briefs.headline: Homebuilders Post Session's Worst Sector Loss Amid Rate Uncertainty
briefs.body: The Homebuilders group fell more than three percent on Friday, the steepest sector decline of the session by a considerable margin, with CSW Industrials — tied to building products — shedding 7.12 percent as well. The outsized losses reflect persistent sensitivity to borrowing-cost expectations at a moment when the interest-rate outlook remains unsettled.
